title: Problem restoring database from BACKUP/RESTORE with S3 Plain Rewritable Disk: UUID mismatch and empty tables
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
After restoring from a backup to S3 using s3_plain_rewritable, the database and tables are created with new UUIDs instead of the original ones. As a result, the tables appear empty because their data on S3 is linked to the old UUIDs.
Attempts to manually specify the original UUID or use ATTACH DATABASE ... UUID ... result in a logical error about missing UUID mapping. This makes it impossible to restore data access after backup in this setup.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
ClickHouse 25.9.3
### How to reproduce
External disk configured as s3_plain_rewritable:
```xml
<storage_configuration>
<disks>
<s3_plain_rewritable>
<type>object_storage</type>
<object_storage_type>s3</object_storage_type>
<metadata_type>plain_rewritable</metadata_type>
<endpoint>http://minio:9000/clickhouse-data/</endpoint>
<access_key_id>minioadmin</access_key_id>
<secret_access_key>minioadmin</secret_access_key>
<use_environment_credentials>0</use_environment_credentials>
<region>us-east-1</region>
</s3_plain_rewritable>
</disks>
<policies>
<s3_policy>
<volumes>
<s3_plain_rewritable>
<disk>s3_plain_rewritable</disk>
</s3_plain_rewritable>
</volumes>
</s3_policy>
</policies>
</storage_configuration>
<merge_tree>
<storage_policy>s3_policy</storage_policy>
</merge_tree>
```
Database created as:
```sql
CREATE DATABASE db1 ENGINE = Atomic SETTINGS disk = 's3_plain_rewritable';
CREATE TABLE db1.test_table (
id UInt32,
name String,
created_at DateTime
) ENGINE = MergeTree()
ORDER BY (id, created_at)
SETTINGS storage_policy = 's3_policy';
INSERT INTO db1.test_table VALUES
(1, 'test1', now()),
(2, 'test2', now()),
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE db1.test_table_v2 (id UInt32, name String, created_at DateTime, is_active UInt8 DEFAULT 1) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY (id, created_at);
INSERT INTO db1.test_table_v2 VALUES (3, 'test3', now(), 1), (4, 'test4', now(), NULL);
CREATE TABLE db1.test_table_v3 (id UInt32, name String, created_at DateTime, is_active UInt8) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY (id, created_at);
INSERT INTO db1.test_table_v3 SELECT id, name, created_at, is_active FROM db1.test_table_v2 WHERE is_active = 1;
SELECT id, name, created_at FROM db1.test_table_v3 WHERE created_at > now() - INTERVAL '1' DAY;
key_bug_features: Restoring database from S3 causes UUID mismatch
